extraction.one_line_summary: STUYVEN Jasper won from 3-rider breakaway, dropping CORT on Bosberg 2km out, then overpowering VAN DER POEL in 2-up sprint
tactical_patterns: Crosswind echelon split after second Leberg passage created decisive front group; favorites' group lost 90+ seconds irretrievably
STUYVEN Jasper won despite being forced to lead out sprint; pure power overcame positional disadvantage against VAN DER POEL
Quick-Step placed DECLERCQ Tim in break for domestique insurance; isolated VAN AERT/VAN AVERMAET responded too late to echelon

rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: first race since Tour 2019 crash yet easily strongest on Molenberg's steep section, dropped entire break group
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: had ideal sprint position trailing STUYVEN but lacked power to come around, reaction slow when attacked
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: missed critical echelon selection despite being race favorite, never bridged 90-second gap despite chasing
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: chased solo from favorites to break but couldn't close final gap, wasted energy with no team support
rider_signals.DECLERCQ Tim: positive: Quick-Step tactical placement in break paid off with 5th place, showing team depth in coverage tactics
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: neutral: hung with leaders until Bosberg attack 2km out, tactical positioning solid but lacked final punch
